title: eriocarpum blephariphyllum
intro: Eriocarpum blephariphyllum, also known as the hairy-fruited Erione, is a small, shrub-like plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is primarily found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be attributed to its high concentration of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Eriocarpum blephariphyllum has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption.
